mnemonics: SORTED: Structured, Operational, Real-time, Transient, External, Detailed
keyPoints: Data sources are the original systems from which data is extracted for the data warehouse.
They are often transactional databases (OLTP) designed for day-to-day operations, not analysis.
Sources can be internal (e.g., CRM, ERP) or external (e.g., market data feeds, social media).
commonMistakes: Assuming data from all sources is clean and consistent, leading to ETL errors.
Underestimating the complexity of integrating data from disparate source systems with different formats.
Failing to establish a regular extraction schedule, causing stale or incomplete data in the warehouse.
